assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Check for PDB blocking drain (most common cause)' and mentions 'these cause 80% of stuck drain issues'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' command which is equivalent to checking PDBs across all namespaces

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands to identify blocking pods: checking Terminating/Pending pods, FailedScheduling events, and bare pods without owner references

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl top nodes' and 'kubectl describe nodes' commands to check resource constraints and allocated resources

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Includes specific kubectl patch command to temporarily adjust PDB maxUnavailable to 50% and mentions backing up original PDB

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud command to update node pool with max-surge-upgrade and max-unavailable-upgrade settings as Fix #3

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Includes section about checking admission webhooks with kubectl commands for validatingwebhookconfigurations and mutatingwebhookconfigurations

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Provides 'Monitor Progress' section with watch commands and gcloud operations list to track upgrade progress
